# vite-plugin-vue-import-to-global

Use Vue from `window.Vue` instead of bundling it.

This Vite plugin rewrites Vue imports **after build** so your output uses the global
`window.Vue` runtime. It is designed for environments where Vue is already provided
externally.

---

## Use cases

- Vue loaded via CDN
- Micro-frontend architectures
- CMS / third-party embeds
- Avoiding duplicate Vue runtimes
- Library builds that must not bundle Vue

---

## Installation

```bash
npm install vite-plugin-vue-import-to-global
```

## Usage

Add the plugin to your `vite.config.ts`:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from "vite"
import vue from "@vitejs/plugin-vue"
import vueImportToGlobal from "vite-plugin-vue-import-to-global"

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    vue(),
    vueImportToGlobal()
  ],
  build: {
    rollupOptions: {
      external: ["vue"] // REQUIRED
    }
  }
})
```

## Runtime requirement (IMPORTANT)

You must provide Vue globally at runtime.

### Example (CDN)

```html
<script type="module">
  import * as Vue from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vue@latest/dist/vue.esm-browser.prod.js"
  window.Vue = Vue
</script>
```

Or using the global build:

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/vue@latest/dist/vue.global.prod.js"></script>
```

## What this plugin does

After build, it converts Vue imports like:

```ts
import { createApp, ref } from "vue"
```

into:

```ts
const { createApp, ref } = window.Vue
```

This ensures:

- Vue is not bundled
- Vue is resolved from `window.Vue`
- Your output works in CDN / embed environments

## Notes & limitations

- Vue must NOT be bundled
- `vue` must be marked as `external`
- `window.Vue` must exist before your code runs
- This plugin is intended for library / embed builds, not standard SPA apps
